# Data Quality Report — D2C Churn Capstone (Part 1)

**Snapshot date:** 2025-09-30  |  **Customer universe:** 2,400  |  Generated by `eda_audit.ipynb`.

## 1. Row counts (match data dictionary)

| File | Rows | Cols |
|---|---|---|
| customers | 2,400 | 9 |
| orders | 10,009 | 10 |
| support_tickets | 1,921 | 8 |
| web_events_snapshot | 2,400 | 10 |
| churn_labels | 2,400 | 4 |
| rfm_modeling_snapshot | 2,400 | 29 |
| intervention_history | 2,400 | 5 |

## 2. Issues found & recommended treatment

| Issue | Location | Magnitude | Treatment |
|---|---|---|---|
| Duplicate-like orders | `orders.order_id` (`*_DUP`) | 12 rows, full-content duplicates | Drop `_DUP` rows before any order aggregation |
| Missing `loyalty_tier` | `customers` | 1,386 (58%) | Map null -> "Not enrolled" (informative category) |
| Missing `skin_type` | `customers` | 401 (17%) | Map null -> "Unknown"; minimal analytic value |
| Missing `rating` | `orders` | 80 | Exclude from rating means; do NOT zero-fill |
| Outlier `gross_amount` | `orders` | 5 rows > INR 10k (max INR 24,789) | Winsorise at p99 (INR 2,309) for monetary features |
| Post-snapshot orders | `orders.order_date` | 1,872 rows | LABEL ONLY — exclude from features (leakage) |

## 3. Join / key integrity
- All foreign keys (`customer_id`) in orders, tickets, web, churn, interventions resolve to the customer universe — **no orphans**.
- All 2,400 customers have >=1 pre-snapshot order; only 1,247 (52%) have a support ticket.
- **Use left joins from `customers`**; absent tickets are expected, not errors.

## 4. Date consistency
- signup_date: 2024-01-01 -> 2025-09-15
- order_date: 2024-01-09 -> 2025-11-29 (extends past snapshot by design)
- ticket_date: 2024-01-13 -> 2025-09-30 (never crosses snapshot)
- Orders dated before the customer's signup: 0 (none).

## 5. Leakage columns (critical)
- `orders` rows with `order_date > 2025-09-30` (1,872 rows) and the `churn_next_60d` target itself must never enter the feature set.
- Recommended modeling base: `rfm_modeling_snapshot.csv`, which the dictionary certifies as leakage-safe.
